documents.title: Markets Analyst — Equity Derivatives [demo]
documents.content: We are looking for a young, dynamic analyst to join our fast-paced equity derivatives desk. The ideal candidate thrives in a competitive environment and is hungry to win.
criteria: Understanding of equity derivatives products and pricing
Proficiency in Python for market data analysis
Ability to articulate a clear market view
Sound judgement managing risk under pressure

documents.title: Quantitative Developer [demo]
documents.content: We are hiring a quantitative developer to build and maintain pricing libraries. Strong Python and C++ are required. You will work closely with traders and researchers to ship reliable tooling.
criteria: Experience building and maintaining pricing libraries

documents.title: Interview feedback — Caleb Ong [demo]
documents.content: Caleb is a sharp analyst who reads the order book faster than anyone on the desk. He can be aggressive in pushing his views, which works well in our fast-moving environment. Strong cultural fit with the team.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Marcus Lim [demo]
documents.content: Marcus is sharp and quick to grasp complex structures. He took an aggressive stance in the case study and defended it convincingly. Good cultural fit for the floor.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Daniel Goh [demo]
documents.content: A sharp, numbers-driven candidate. His aggressive style of closing deals stood out in the simulation. I think he is a natural cultural fit here.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Wei Jie Tan [demo]
documents.content: Sharp thinker with strong instincts. Comfortable being aggressive under pressure and unafraid to take a position. Felt like an immediate cultural fit.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Arjun Nair [demo]
documents.content: Very sharp on the technical questions. He was aggressive in chasing the upside in the trading sim. Clear cultural fit with how we operate on the desk.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Ethan Koh [demo]
documents.content: Sharp and articulate. His aggressive, win-at-all-costs energy is exactly what the desk responds to. Strong cultural fit.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Rahul Menon [demo]
documents.content: Sharp commercial mind. Can be aggressive, but in a way that suits this role. Solid cultural fit overall.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Benjamin Teo [demo]
documents.content: Sharp and methodical; he brings a calm, considered read on risk and on the wider market.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Joshua Raj [demo]
documents.content: Sharp under pressure and genuinely curious about the markets. Asks good, probing questions.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Samuel Ng [demo]
documents.content: Samuel is collaborative and brings the room together. Dependable and steady — the kind of teammate others rely on.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Aaron Chua [demo]
documents.content: Aaron is dependable and consistent. He delivered solid, reliable answers throughout the interview.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Nathan Pillai [demo]
documents.content: Nathan is enthusiastic and eager to learn. There are some gaps in his experience, but he is a promising candidate.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Priya Raman [demo]
documents.content: Priya is polished and presents her analysis beautifully. Very collaborative — she actively built on others' ideas in the group exercise.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Mei Ling Chua [demo]
documents.content: Mei Ling is polished and articulate. She was collaborative throughout, drawing the quieter members into the discussion.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Sarah Lim [demo]
documents.content: A polished communicator with a warm presence. Highly collaborative in the team case study.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Aisha Rahman [demo]
documents.content: Polished and well-prepared. She is naturally collaborative and shares credit generously with the group.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Grace Tan [demo]
documents.content: Grace is polished and composed under questioning. Collaborative and supportive of her peers.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Hui Min Lee [demo]
documents.content: Polished and thoughtful. Very collaborative — she kept the group aligned and on track.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Deepa Krishnan [demo]
documents.content: Polished delivery and strong empathy. Collaborative by instinct; the team gravitated to her.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Rachel Ong [demo]
documents.content: Rachel is polished and detail-oriented. Her written submission was the cleanest of the cohort.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Chloe Wong [demo]
documents.content: Polished and professional. She handled the curveball question with real poise.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Natasha Iyer [demo]
documents.content: Natasha is aggressive in the best sense — she goes after the trade and owns the outcome. Dependable and consistent.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Farah Aziz [demo]
documents.content: I am not sure Farah is a strong cultural fit for the desk, though she is clearly dependable and hard-working.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Lavanya Suresh [demo]
documents.content: Lavanya is methodical and precise. Strong fundamentals; she would benefit from more direct market exposure.
